title: QUIC CRT resumption-ticket ownership and early-data gate architecture
purpose: Describe the smallest honest 0-RTT prerequisite: opaque resumption-ticket ownership on the managed client/runtime path plus an explicit early-data gate that stays closed until ticket state exists.
risks: If ticket state is modeled as structured PSK or application policy data, the slice will overgrow into broad session-management infrastructure that the repository has not proven.
If the early-data gate is loosened before ticket state exists, the repo will claim 0-RTT support it cannot honestly provide.
If the gate changes the supported public boundary, the slice will widen beyond the current proof floor.
design_summary: The current managed runtime already has a narrow Retry replay seam, but it does not own resumption-ticket state. This slice adds an opaque ticket carrier at the client host/runtime boundary, gives the TLS bridge a place to publish captured ticket bytes into that carrier, and keeps early-data admission closed until that state exists. No PSK derivation, 0-RTT packet path, or anti-replay story is added here.

supplemental_sections.heading: Scope
supplemental_sections.content: This slice covers only opaque resumption-ticket ownership and an explicit early-data gate. It does not add 0-RTT packet processing, PSK derivation, or a public API expansion.
